item.title: Cabinet committee on law-order formed
item.description: The government has formed a cabinet committee with home minister Salahuddin Ahmed as its convener to monitor, review and improve the overall law and order situation......

item.title: GAU develops high drought-tolerant soya bean variety
item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University has achieved a landmark breakthrough in climate-resilient agriculture with the development of the country’s first high drought-tolerant inbred soya bean variety, ‘GAU Soybean 6’......

item.title: 2 sweetmeats shops fined in Sylhet
item.description: Sylhet divisional office of the Directorate of National Consumer Rights Protection and the Sylhet district administration in separate drives fined two sweetmeats shops in the city on Wednesday......

item.title: Canals to be recovered from occupants: Anee
item.description: Water resources minister Md Shahiduddin Chowdhury Anee on Wednesday said that canals would be recovered from illegal occupants......

item.title: ACC seizes, freezes assets worth Tk 2,271cr in Jan
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission seized and froze assets worth Tk 2,271 crore at home and abroad in January this year following court orders......

item.title: Missing child found dead in Narsingdi pond
item.description: The police recovered the body of an eight-year-old child from a pond in Narsingdi on Wednesday morning, 20 hours after he went missing......

item.title: Travel ban on former DMP chief Habibur, 3 SBAC Bank officials
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday imposed a travel ban on former commissioner of Dhaka Metropolitan Police Habibur Rahman and three officials of South Bangla Agriculture and Commerce Bank PLC over graft allegations......

item.title: Man found chained 4 days after ‘abduction’
item.description: The police rescued a chained man in Darshana upazila of Chuadanga district, four days after he was reportedly abducted......

item.title: Govt takes up plans to combat mosquito menace: minister
item.description: Health and family welfare minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Hossain on Wednesday said that the government had undertaken various plans to free the city dwellers from the mosquito menace......

item.title: Coast Guard arrests 9 ‘robbers’, rescues 4 fishermen in Cox’s Bazar
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard in an operation arrested the ringleader of a robber group named ‘Jahangir Bahini’ and his eight associates along with weapons and ammunition at Maheskhali in Cox’s Bazar on Tuesday......

item.title: 30 injured as NCP lawmaker comes under attack at Hatiya
item.description: Over 30 people, including two police personnel, were injured as supporters of National Citizen Party clashed with the Bangladesh Nationalist Party and Bangladesh Awami League activists in Hatiya upazila of Noakhali on Wednesday......

item.title: Two on-duty traffic officers beaten in Chattogram
item.description: Two traffic police officials were beaten and injured by a dump truck owner while on duty in Fatikchhari upazila of Chattogram on Wednesday......

item.title: ACC sues ex-addl SP Uttam Kumar, wife
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission filed two separate cases against retired additional superintendent of police Uttam Kumar Biswas and his wife Krishna Biswas on charges of amassing wealth beyond their known sources of income and money laundering......

item.title: SEU signs memo with South Korea’s Juyoung Bio
item.description: The department of pharmacy and Southeast Pharmacy Club of Southeast University signed a memorandum of understanding with the Juyoung Bio Inc, South Korea at the vice-chancellor conference room of the university in Dhaka on Sunday......

item.title: People besiege Hatirjheel police station demanding arrest of perpetrators
item.description: Agitated people besieged the Hatirjheel police station on Wednesday evening, demanding justice over the death of a girl under the police station jurisdiction on Tuesday......

item.title: Dhaka, KL to boost religious ties
item.description: Bangladesh and Malaysia have agreed to strengthen cooperation in the field of religious affairs......

item.title: Birth, death registration hampered at places
item.description: The birth and death registration process has been disrupted in several parts of the country over the past few days, with local authorities blaming server-related problems......

item.title: New IGP takes charge, DMP chief Sazzat Ali resigns
item.description: Dhaka Metropolitan Police commissioner Sheikh Md Sazzat Ali resigned from his post while the newly appointed inspector general of police Md Ali Hossain Fakir took charge at the office on Wednesday......

item.title: 12 lawyers sued, bar chief sent to jail
item.description: An additional metropolitan magistrate court in Barishal on Wednesday sent Barishal District Bar Association president Sadiqur Rahman Lincoln to jail after his arrest in a case filed against 12 lawyers over alleged courtroom vandalism......

item.title: Victim families for publishing perpetrators’ names
item.description: The families of the victims of the 2009 Bangladesh Rifles mutiny at the erstwhile BDR headquarters in the capital’s Pilkhana area on Wednesday urged the government to publish the names of the perpetrators and conspirators mentioned in the National Independent Investigation Commission report......

item.title: 5 lakh farmer cards in 180 days
item.description: The government would pilot the distribution of five lakh krishak smart cards among farmers in 15 upazilas in the next 180 days under a 2023–2028 development programme......

item.title: Ekushey Book Fair begins today
item.description: The Amar Ekushey Book Fair 2026 will begin on the Bangla Academy premises and at Suhrawardy Udyan in the capital Dhaka today, with stall preparations yet to be finished......

item.title: NCP demands president’s impeachment, arrest
item.description: National Citizen Party convener and opposition whip Md Nahid Islam on Wednesday demanded the impeachment and arrest of president Mohammed Shahabuddin for the latter’s alleged inaction during the July 24 mass killing......

item.title: PM pledges to modernise, consolidate border force
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Wednesday said that his government would further modernise and consolidate the border force so that its members could carry out their duties with patriotism and professional excellence......

item.title: America’s dangerous gamble in Cuba
item.description: THE streets of Havana, once alive with music and the hum of daily life, now tell a quieter story: one of long lines at gas stations that have nothing to dispense, hospitals flickering in and out of power, and garbage piling in corners because the trucks that once cleared it lack the fuel to move. This is not the aftermath of a natural disaster or a civil war. It is the result of......

item.title: Coming aesthetics of tricksterial deconstruction
item.description: ART is a phenomenal experience in life. Many a connoisseur considers it an allegory of a pleasurable journey. The taste of art is found in an alternative way of living for those who believe that what we taste as art is a rediscovery of a tasteful mind, which warrants a question as to how art is created. And, there is another relevant question: how does creativity transform an......

item.title: Preferring individuals to institutions
item.description: RESEARCH evidence shows that in a politically polarised state, preference for individual leadership to institution building and reform is a primary hindrance to ensuring good governance. Douglass North identified institutions as humanly devised constraints that structure political, economic and social interactions wherein formal rules: constitutions, laws, property rights......Breaking news alerts

item.title: Can BNP transform victory into real change?
item.description: BANGLADESH, a nation shaped by a history of resilience and struggle, has reached a defining crossroads. The results of the 13th national parliamentary election have handed the Bangladesh Nationalist Party an overwhelming victory — a triumph that not only marks a watershed moment in the country’s political history but also sets the stage for a crucial test......

item.title: Early, effective measures needed to contain dengue
item.description: A SHARP increase in mosquito density inDhakais a public health alarm that the authorities can ill afford to ignore. Residents of neighbourhoods from Mirpur to Uttara report the increase in mosquito menace over the past few weeks, suggesting that vector control efforts fall short. The convergence of favourable weather, poor waste management and clogged drains has......

item.title: BNP should look into why grass-roots fails to learn
item.description: AN EIGHT-YEAR-OLD boy died after a vehicle of the convoy of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party member of parliament for the Cox’s Bazar 2 constituency had hit him at Chakaria on February 22. When the lawmaker was on his way back from Kutubdia in the afternoon, the vehicle, which was at the tail end of the convoy, hit the boy, a student of Class II in a madrassah......Top news summaries

item.title: 13th JS polls not entirely beyond question: SHUJAN
item.description: Civil society organisation Sushasaner Jonno Nagorik on Wednesday said that the 13th 13th Jatiya Sangsad elections and the referendum were partly free from controversy......

item.title: EC to document activities of foreign election observers
item.description: The Election Commission has formed a 33-member committee to prepare comprehensive documentation on the activities of foreign observers who participated in the 13th Jatiya Sangsad elections held on February 12......

item.title: EC asks 13th JS candidates to file election expenses by March 15
item.description: The Election Commission has instructed all candidates who contested the 13th Jatiya Sangsad elections to submit their election expenditure statements by March 15......

item.title: Bir Shreshtho Nur Mohammad’s 90th birth anniv today
item.description: The 90th birth anniversary of Bir Shreshtho Nur Mohammad Sheikh will be observed on Thursday with due respect and solemnity in Narail......

item.title: Man shot dead at Raujan
item.description: A man was shot dead by unidentified assailants in the Oli Mia Hat area of Raujan in Chattogram Wednesday evening......

item.title: Jamaat concerned over president’s disclosure of delicate issues
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Wednesday expressed concern over the disclosure of some important and delicate matters of the state by president Mohammed Shahabuddin in an interview with a Bangla daily which was published recently......

item.title: Iran rejects US claims on missile programme as ‘big lies’
item.description: Iran’s foreign ministry on Wednesday dismissed US claims about its missile programme as ‘big lies’, after president Donald Trump said Tehran was developing missiles that can strike the United States......

item.title: Myanmar junta air strike kills 17
item.description: A Myanmar military air strike on a village market in the country’s westernmost state killed at least 17 people, two local sources said on Wednesday......

item.title: Trump tries to reset presidency in State of the Union speech
item.description: US president Donald Trump boasted Tuesday of a ‘turnaround for the ages’ in his State of the Union speech, seeking to reverse dismal polls and see off mounting challenges at home and abroad ahead of crucial midterm elections......

item.title: 129 media professionals killed worldwide in 2025
item.description: A record 129 journalists and media workers were killed worldwide in 2025, the Committee to Protect Journalists said Wednesday, blaming Israel for two-thirds of the deaths......

item.title: 68,767 students awarded junior scholarships
item.description: After a five-year hiatus, a total of 68,767 students received scholarships under the 2025 Junior Scholarship Examinations......

item.title: Seven charged over fake certificate used for 20yrs
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission on Wednesday submitted chargesheet in a case filed against a high official of the national housing authority for securing job with a fake certificate and six others for their involvement with it......
